tasks.name: preparePublicKeyForDeployment + pkToNttCompact + G5 KAT (A-007 structural oracle)
G6 happy-path validateUserOp + gas-cap + AC-FLOW-1 (100 KAT + 5 fresh-keypair)

patternsEstablished: A-007 structural-reconciliation oracle for G5 pk-transform: fixture encodes as uint256[32] (1024 B, Python ref sig_sol.py:48), preparePublicKeyForDeployment emits uint256[] (1088 B, on-chain setKey input). Test decodes both sides with decodeAbiParameters and compares element-wise bigint equality. Precedent: test/signers/mldsa-encoding.pk-transform.kat.test.ts:146-204.

preparePublicKeyForDeployment signature is single-factory for falcon-eth (keccakXofFactory only, accepted for NFR-11 symmetry but unused internally because falcon's forward-NTT is deterministic over raw pk). Distinct from ml-dsa-eth's two-factory binding (keccakXofFactory, keccakXofFactory). pkToNttCompact delegates to encodePublicKeyForZKNOX then decodes the uint256[] wrapper to recover the bigint[] shape.

FalconEthAccount.sol: 127 LOC structural mirror of MlDsaEthAccount.sol; wrapper-path import `./imports/FalconRef.sol` (NOT direct ETHFALCON/src/); _VERIFY_SELECTOR = bytes4(keccak256('verify(bytes,bytes32,bytes)')) — ADL-ambiguity-safe; try/catch → SignatureMalformed() on verifier revert; SIG_VALIDATION_FAILED on crypto failure. Dual-path walker (T5) will bind to the errorName on accountAddress.
FalconRef.sol SHA (9043bb6...9c) byte-preserved across T1/T2/T3 (AC-11 + AC-14 regression guards). No submodule-immutability violation.

test/fixtures/falcon-eth.ts: 98 LOC mirror of test/fixtures/mldsa-eth.ts; two-step simulate+write for setKey (viem typed-contract pattern); 20-byte SSTORE2 pointer-length check; cross-fixture network-sharing pattern (optional viem parameter to avoid HH3 EdrProvider isolation). registerPublicKey IS the T4/T5 entry point.
Shared test helpers come from test/utils/assert-bytes.ts; module-scope divergence-message helpers (no inline redefinition per retrospect/typescript.md §'Duplicated test-file code').
deviations: Rule 3 (T1): A-007 amendment — AC-1 oracle shape changed from raw Hex byte-equality to structural coefficient-equality. Architecture anticipated the divergence-probe fallback at docs/architecture.md:199 ('pick structural-decode oracle up front'). T1 code preserved as-is; test rewritten to structural decode; AC text + must_have truth amended inline; sweep grep clean post-commit.
openIssues: Pre-existing inaccuracy (out of 2-4 scope): docs/stories/1-1.md:342 describes fixture reshapedPublicKey as 'abi-encoded uint256[]' — actual layout is uint256[32] per scripts/generate-kat-fixtures.ts:1304 + A-007. Flagged in A-007; retrospective amendment of signed-off Story 1-1 is out of scope.

tasks.name: Fork apply + repo wiring + falcon512paddedEth instantiation

patternsEstablished: Strategy E fork-injection: our repo instantiates `falcon512paddedEth = genFalcon({ ...falcon512paddedOpts, hashToPoint: hashToPointEVM })`; fork exports only `genFalcon` + `falcon512paddedOpts` (NOT base `falcon512opts`).
BytesReader interface in falcon-eth.kat-internal.ts abstracts signing randomness — KAT uses rngAesCtrDrbg256-driven reader, production uses crypto.getRandomValues-backed reader.
SIGNING_BYTES_EXHAUSTED guard pattern at randomness-source boundary — present in both `signWithKatBytes` (KAT) and `signUserOp` (production) callbacks; matches errors.ts JSDoc.

AC-7 boundary mirrors ml-dsa-eth: falcon-eth.ts (production) and falcon-eth.kat-internal.ts (KAT) do NOT import each other; both route through falcon-eth.core.js (falcon512paddedEth) + falcon-encoding.js (encodeSignatureForZKNOX).

Two-repo commit discipline: fork commits staged + committed locally, user pushes, our-repo commits land the pin. Branch pin (#falcon-eth-hashtopoint-injection) per user instruction; no merge to fork main; work-in-progress posture.
deviations: Rule 1 (T2): renamed falcon512Eth → falcon512paddedEth mid-commit to match noble's falcon512padded naming convention (our variant extends padded, not unpadded). Sweep across 6 files in the T2 commit.

Rule 2 (T2 Step A): fork diff raw shortstat is 33+/27- = 60 LOC vs story's ≤25 bound; semantic is ~7 new LOC (the 27/27 is re-indentation of preserved 25-line SHAKE256 body). Accepted; logged for Gate 5.
Rule 1 (T2 Step A): falcon512paddedOpts got explicit ': FalconOpts' annotation due to fork's `isolatedDeclarations: true` tsconfig setting.

Rule 2 (T3): falcon-eth.ts#signUserOp does NOT import from kat-internal (AC-7 boundary). Prompt had it routing through signWithKatBytes; corrected to mirror ml-dsa-eth.ts pattern — production calls falcon512paddedEth.sign + encodeSignatureForZKNOX directly.
Rule 2 (T3): signWithKatBytes guard also checks returned-chunk length (not just requested length) so AC-5 SIGNING_BYTES_EXHAUSTED has a testable path via a buggy reader.
Rule 1 (T3 revise): signUserOp guard added to close the JSDoc-vs-behavior gap on errors.ts documentation; defense-in-depth.
openIssues: T2 Step A: fork raw diff 60 LOC exceeds story Gate-5 sanity check #3 (≤25). Semantic diff ~7 LOC; relies on `git diff -w` for audit. Flag at Gate 5.
T3: signUserOp SIGNING_BYTES_EXHAUSTED guard is present but structurally untestable on the happy path (fixed 88 B buffer + noble's 88 B consumption). Covered defensively; no independent unit test.
Fork branch `falcon-eth-hashtopoint-injection` is pinned (not merged to main); work-in-progress posture per user instruction. ml-dsa-eth migration to the same fork deferred to a separate ops task.
